steps.title: Read the vertex directly from vertex form
steps.content: $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x-7)^2 + 3$ is in vertex form $a(x-h)^2 + k$. Vertex is $(h, k) = (7, 3)$.

steps.title: Vertex shortcut: $a \gt 0$ means vertex is the minimum
steps.content: $a = \frac{1}{9} \gt 0$, so the parabola opens upward — the vertex $(7, 3)$ is the minimum. The minimum height is $3$ inches. That matches choice A — A is correct.
